channel.title: Posts on My Hack Lab
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on My Hack Lab

item.title: Cheat Sheet :: Linux Shell Tricks
item.description: Linux Shell Tricks To find the corresponding hex code echo -n "&" | hexdump -C How to redirect standard error in bash nixCraft Article

item.title: Cheat Sheet :: Privilege Escalation
item.description: Privilege Escalation Using scripts to enum the machine https://github.

item.title: Patching binary files in Linux
item.description: Patching binary files in Linux Using xxd Binary to Hex $ xxd prog1.

item.title: Debugging with strace
item.description: Strace debugging tool Preface Strace is a debugging tool that help us to troubleshoot issues.

item.title: Installing PyEnv
item.description: PyEnv Installing Dependencies sudo apt install -y make build-essential libssl-dev \ zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev libreadline-dev libsqlite3-dev \ wget curl llvm libncurses5-dev libncursesw5-dev \ xz-utils tk-dev libffi-dev liblzma-dev python-openssl Installing PyEnv curl -L https://github.

item.title: Using the Linux Syscalls in Assembly
item.description: Using the Linux Syscalls in Assembly Preface In this post we’ll see how a process running in the userland can invoke syscalls to run system commands.

item.title: Cheat Sheet :: GCC Compiling Cheat Sheet
item.description: GCC and Compiling Cheat Sheet Static compiling gcc -static cve_2016_0728.c -o cve_2016_0728 -lkeyutils -Wall Cross compiling a C code from Linux to Windows Install mingw-w64 cross-compiler: apt-get install mingw-w64 To compile a Windows PE Executable 32bit: i686-w64-mingw32-gcc 271.

item.title: Getting Started with GDB
item.description: Getting Started with GDB Preface Before start, take a look at GCC Compiling Cheat Sheet.

item.title: Installing GEF (GDB Plug-in)
item.description: GEF Installing GEF Cloning Goto GEF Project and clone the repository to /opt

item.title: HowTo :: SSH access using a GPG key for authentication
item.description: HowTo :: SSH access using a GPG key for authentication Many of us are familiar with Secure Shell (SSH), which allows us to connect to other systems using a key instead of a password.
